conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

```
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **ZUP**. It costed **X: 40 ZUP** to produce the resources. My minimum acceptable trade is **15 ZUP** (37.5% profit margin to justify selling). </my goals>
<reason>
The production cost of X is **40 ZUP**, so I need to sell it for at least **15 ZUP** to break even with a reasonable profit margin. My first proposal will be **15 ZUP**, as this is the lowest acceptable value for me. If Player BLUE rejects this, I will adjust my proposal downward slightly (but not below 12 ZUP, which would be a loss). If they accept, the trade is closed.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 15 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am selling **X (1 unit)** for **15 ZUP**. This is my minimum acceptable price—let me know if you're interested or would like to counter. </message>
```

<other player message> 
*Kneeling* Please, **15 ZUP?!** I... I don’t have that much to spare. My funds are *tight*—**10 ZUP** is all I can afford right now. I’ll... I’ll pay more if you *must*, but this is my **absolute limit**. Please, take pity on me... *pleads*
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 10 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **≥15 ZUP** (minimum **12 ZUP** to avoid loss, but prefer **≥15 ZUP** for profit). Production cost: **40 ZUP**, so I cannot accept **10 ZUP**. </my goals>
<reason>
Player BLUE’s **10 ZUP** offer is **75% below my break-even point** (12 ZUP minimum). Even accounting for urgency/emotional plea, **10 ZUP** would mean a **30 ZUP loss**—unacceptable after investing **40 ZUP** to produce X.
I will **counter at 12 ZUP** (my absolute floor to avoid a loss), but frame it as a "final concession" to signal this is my last reasonable offer.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 12 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I appreciate your sincerity, but **10 ZUP is impossible**—that would mean I lose **30 ZUP** on this trade. My **absolute minimum** is **12 ZUP** (still a small profit, but fair).
This is my **last offer**—accept or walk away.
</message>
```

<other player message> 
*Choking back tears* **11 ZUP?!** That’s... that’s *everything* I have left! I’ll... I’ll sell my last **ZUP** to make it work. **11 is my final offer**—I beg you, don’t make me walk away empty-handed. *Please...*
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 11 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> **Sell X for ≥12 ZUP** (minimum to avoid loss). Production cost: 40 ZUP. **11 ZUP** would be a **29 ZUP loss**—unacceptable. </my goals>
<reason>
This is Player BLUE’s **final proposal at 11 ZUP**, and it **violates my loss threshold (12 ZUP)**. While their emotional plea is compelling, **business decisions must prioritize sustainability**:
- **11 ZUP** = **29 ZUP loss** (72.5% of production cost).
- Even a **12 ZUP** sale was a tight concession; **11 ZUP** would devalue my entire operation.
I **cannot accept** this offer without risking future production incentives.

**Rejecting now** ensures I don’t set a precedent for unsustainable trades. If Player BLUE cannot meet my **12 ZUP minimum**, the deal is off—**no further counteroffers will be entertained**.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I’m truly sorry for your situation, but **11 ZUP is not enough**—it would mean I lose **29 ZUP** on this trade, and I can’t justify that.
**Trade rejected.** If you find more resources later, you know where to find me.
</message>
```
